title: Evaluating SLA Policies
concept: Defining service level agreements for agent tasks: max run time, max cost, and escalation thresholds.

exercises.prompt: What helps avoid common production pitfalls in AI agents?
exercises.explain: Using logging and circuit breakers helps prevent common issues in production.

cards.title: What is an SLA?
cards.body: A Service Level Agreement (SLA) is a promise between a company and its clients. It defines the standards and performance metrics expected from the service.

cards.title: Key SLA Components
cards.body: SLAs often include max run time, max cost, and escalation thresholds to manage and improve service performance.

cards.title: SLA is like a Recipe
cards.body: Just as a recipe outlines cook time and ingredient costs, SLAs specify how long tasks should take and how much they can cost.

cards.title: Example SLA Parameters
cards.body: An SLA might say an AI task must complete in under 2 seconds and cost no more than $1 per request.

cards.title: Common Mistake
cards.body: Some think SLAs are optional, but they are essential for clear expectations and accountability.

cards.title: SLA Parameters Comparison
cards.body: Compare SLA components to clear expectations in a project.

exercises.prompt: What is a common component of SLAs?
exercises.explain: Max run time is key to ensuring tasks finish on time, making it a vital element of SLAs.

exercises.prompt: SLAs are optional agreements between service providers and clients.
exercises.explain: SLAs are not optional; they are necessary for setting clear service expectations.

exercises.prompt: An SLA sets expectations for maximum _____ and cost.
exercises.sentence: An SLA sets expectations for maximum run time and cost.
exercises.explain: 'Run time' is an important part of the SLA that specifies how long a task should take.

exercises.prompt: Which of these describes an SLA accurately?

options.text: A formalized agreement outlining service expectations
A reminder for social media posts

exercises.explain: The correct choice describes an SLA correctly as a formalized document that outlines service expectations.

exercises.prompt: Which is NOT a parameter of an SLA?
exercises.explain: Agent task preferences are not defined in SLAs; they focus on performance metrics like max run time, cost, and escalation.
